prevalence.trend: increasing diagnosed prevalence due to awareness and lab testing

burden.dalyPerCapita: very low (rare) but high per-patient morbidity; laryngeal attacks can be fatal
burden.mortalityRate: Low with access to on-demand therapy; higher if untreated
burden.source: HAE registry outcomes; WHO/IHME acute airway risk context

riskFactors: ACE inhibitor exposure (can mimic/worsen angioedema; avoid in HAE)
vulnerablePopulations: patients without access to C1-INH testing
patients in remote areas without on-demand therapy
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Recurrent angioedema without urticaria; low C4 and low/absent C1 inhibitor level or function; confirm with repeat labs and genetics when available

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; cascade testing in families when index case diagnosed
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; improves with on-demand therapy and prophylaxis access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Specialized complement testing concentrated in larger labs; family screening may be delayed
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: On-demand therapy (C1-INH concentrate or bradykinin receptor antagonist where available), short/long-term prophylaxis in select patients, trigger avoidance, emergency plan
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Delayed diagnosis and uneven access to high-cost on-demand therapies
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Clear diagnostic biomarkers (C4/C1-INH) and strong impact of appropriate therapy once available
